criteria.criterion: scripts/lib/lower.sh defines a function lower that prints its first argument with ASCII A-Z mapped to a-z.
criteria.evidence: Diff adds scripts/lib/lower.sh lines 3-6: `lower() { printf '%s' "${1-}" | LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z'; printf '\n'; }` — LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z' maps exactly ASCII uppercase to lowercase, leaving other bytes unchanged.
tests/lower.sh line 20 asserts `assert_lower 'ABC' 'abc'` via command substitution against the sourced helper; orchestrator evidence: `bash tests/lower.sh` exited 0.
tests/lower.sh line 21 asserts `assert_lower 'AbC1' 'abc1'`; tr only translates A-Z so the digit '1' passes through; test run exited 0.
tests/lower.sh line 22 asserts `assert_lower '' ''`; helper uses "${1-}" so a missing/empty arg yields empty output (trailing newline stripped by command substitution); test run exited 0.

criteria.criterion: tests/lower.sh sources the helper and asserts those cases; it exits 0 when all assertions pass.
criteria.evidence: tests/lower.sh lines 2-5: `set -euo pipefail; cd "$(dirname "$0")/.."; source scripts/lib/lower.sh`; assert_lower returns 1 on mismatch which aborts under set -e; all three spec cases asserted at lines 20-22; orchestrator confirms exit 0.

scope_finding: Only scripts/lib/lower.sh (matches in_scope scripts/lib/**) and tests/lower.sh (explicitly in_scope) are touched; no out-of-scope paths (.github/**, specs/**, .orchestrator/**) appear in the diff. Orchestrator scope check: PASS.
regression_finding: Both files are new additions; no existing files are modified or deleted, so no existing behaviour can regress. The function name `lower` could shadow an identically named function in scripts that source it, but nothing in the diff or spec context indicates one exists. None observed.
security_findings: No secrets, credential access, network use, or unsafe shell constructs. Input is passed as a quoted printf argument (not interpolated into a command), tr runs with LC_ALL=C, and needs_network=false is respected. None observed.
reasons: All five acceptance criteria are MET with direct evidence in the diff: the helper implements ASCII-only lowercasing via LC_ALL=C tr 'A-Z' 'a-z', and tests/lower.sh sources it and asserts exactly the three specified cases under set -euo pipefail.
Orchestrator evidence corroborates: integrity PASS, scope PASS, and the spec's test_command `bash tests/lower.sh` exited 0.
Changed paths are strictly within the approved scope and the diff contains only new files, so there is no regression surface and no security concern (no network, secrets, or unsafe shell).
